topic.what: Introduced Type A (autoimmune, corpus) vs Type B (bacterial, antral) classification of chronic atrophic gastritis.
topic.how: Clinical-serological correlation linking parietal cell antibodies and topographic distribution to distinct disease entities.
topic.why_matters: First etiological classification of gastritis — established that gastritis is not one disease but at least two, with different causes and consequences.

good_for: Understanding autoimmune vs infectious gastritis distinction
requires: Serological testing for parietal cell antibodies

novelty: First binary etiological classification of gastritis
Established autoimmune gastritis as distinct entity
Topographic mapping (antral vs corpus) as diagnostic criterion
open_problems: Binary classification too simple — many gastritis types exist
Type B mechanism (H. pylori) not identified until 1982
